aliases_seen: SI engine, gasoline engine, petrol engine
one_line_definition: Internal combustion engine where the fuel-air mixture is ignited by an electric spark plug
Internal combustion engine where combustion is initiated by heat from compressing the intake air alone
Net work per cycle divided by the swept volume; a normalised measure of engine torque independent of size
Ratio of the actual mass of air inducted per cycle to the mass that would fill the swept volume at ambient conditions
Mass of fuel consumed per unit of brake work output; the primary metric of engine fuel efficiency
Ratio of stoichiometric AFR to actual AFR (phi=1 is stoichiometric; phi<1 lean, phi>1 rich)
Spontaneous ignition of the end-gas ahead of the SI engine flame front, producing pressure waves and potential engine damage
Use of exhaust-gas-driven turbine to drive a compressor that increases intake air density above ambient
Thermally formed pollutants (NO, NO2) produced at high combustion temperatures via the Zeldovich chain reactions
Ideal thermodynamic cycle for SI engines: isentropic compression, constant-volume heat addition, isentropic expansion, constant-volume heat rejection
The symmetry of any physical property of a crystal cannot be lower than the symmetry of the crystal's point group
Mathematical object relating physical cause and effect vectors; rank-n tensor has 3^n components in 3D, reduced by symmetry
Generation of electric polarisation by applied mechanical stress in non-centrosymmetric crystals; governed by a third-rank tensor
Temperature-dependent spontaneous electric polarisation in polar crystals; described by a first-rank (vector) tensor
Fourth-rank tensor relating strain to stress; 21 independent components for triclinic, 3 for cubic crystals
Set of symmetry operations (rotations, reflections, inversions) that leave at least one point of the crystal fixed; 32 point groups in 3D
Variation of a material property with direction; contrasts with isotropy (property same in all directions)
Vector whose sign depends on the handedness of the coordinate system (e.g. angular velocity); compatible with centrosymmetric point groups
Linear relationship between voltage across and current through a resistor: V = IR
Complex-valued generalisation of resistance for AC circuits, incorporating resistive and reactive components: Z = R + jX
High-gain differential voltage amplifier IC used with external feedback networks to implement analogue signal-processing functions
Voltage-controlled transistor where gate voltage modulates a conducting channel; dominant in digital ICs due to very low static power
Current-controlled transistor with emitter, base, collector terminals; collector current ≈ β × base current
Latching semiconductor switch triggered by a gate pulse and extinguished only by reducing current below holding current
Product RC giving the characteristic time for exponential charge/discharge of a capacitor through a resistor
KVL: sum of voltages around any closed loop = 0; KCL: sum of currents at any node = 0
Feeding a portion of the output back to the inverting input of an amplifier to stabilise gain and reduce distortion
Frequency at which inductive and capacitive reactances cancel, giving maximum (series) or minimum (parallel) impedance
Force per unit area acting on a cross-section; normal stress perpendicular, shear stress parallel to the surface
Dimensionless measure of deformation: change in length divided by original length (normal strain) or angular change (shear strain)
Ratio of normal stress to normal strain in the elastic region; measure of material stiffness

aliases_seen: moment of inertia of area, I
one_line_definition: Geometric property of a cross-section governing bending stiffness; I = ∫y² dA
Normal stress in a beam cross-section due to bending moment: σ = My/I
Graphical method for finding principal stresses and maximum shear stress from a known 2D stress state
Twisting of a shaft by an applied torque; shear stress τ = Tr/J, where J is the polar second moment of area
Conservation of energy for ideal incompressible flow: p + ½ρv² + ρgh = constant along a streamline
Dimensionless ratio of inertial to viscous forces in a flow: Re = ρvL/μ; governs laminar vs. turbulent transition
Energy is conserved: change in internal energy = heat added minus work done by the system
Entropy of an isolated system cannot decrease; sets an upper limit on heat engine efficiency
Maximum possible efficiency of a heat engine: η = 1 − T_cold/T_hot
Measure of a body's resistance to angular acceleration: I = ∫r² dm
Ratio of output to input angular velocity in a geared system; determines torque multiplication and speed reduction
